source: Stadler — ÖBB NV bid (lost, case study)
client_via: Stadler direct PDM export (CATIA-based) -- not SZAKALFEM-origin, no shared identifiers (see OQ-62 amendment)
product: WC-Kabine 9.3, vollst. (UWC cabin, Artikelnummer 12888726), ÖBB NV order, 47 units quoted, production never started -- bid lost
bid_outcome: lost -- case-study data only, not a won production job
documents_processed: STAR_12888726_-_UWC_rev2.xlsx (raw Stadler PDM Stückliste export, result sheet)

pdm_tree.artikelbenennung1: Einschraubmutter RAMPA Typ B fuer Holz

pdm_tree.artikelbenennung2: Han M12, D-kodiert, Crimp, gewinkelt, sl

pdm_tree.artikelbenennung1: Fuellstutzen mit Sieb AG G1 1/4 Zoll

findings_for_cross_analysis: Zero embedded images in this PDM export type -- unlike Inventor-sourced BOMs, no automatic thumbnail source exists for this product family; any photo would need to come from a separate CAD/PDM viewer export, not this file.

No material/Werkstoff/norm fields exist anywhere in this export type, unlike the SZAKALFEM-origin Stuckliste documents. Material data for this product lives only in the TEHNOLOGIJA technology-route file's Materijal/Opis column, sourced separately by Stirg, not from Stadler's own PDM export.
Artikelstatus is uniformly 'Freigegeben' (released) across all 6524 rows -- an administrative release flag, not a manufacturing-progress signal; not usable as an operations-pipeline status source.

19.1% of rows (1249/6524) carry Stadler's own 'vertraulich' (confidential) flag. Descriptive fields (Artikelbenennung1/2) for those rows are redacted (null) in this public fixture; only structural fields (hierarchy, qty, document/article numbers) are retained. Full descriptive content, if ever needed, belongs in a private fixture, not this public repo.

Hierarchy depth reaches 9 levels (Ebene 0-8), consistent with prior findings (GST: 7 levels, SZAKALFEM Stadler source: 3 levels) that raw nesting depth varies by source and is independent of PPM's 3-tier display model (D-96 collapsing, not raw-data constraint).

Zero overlap confirmed between this file's Artikelnummer/Dokumentnummer identifiers and the 152 Sachnummer values in the existing SZAKALFEM-origin stadler_structured.json fixture -- closes the identifier-connection sub-question raised in OQ-62 (same physical tank product, independently documented by two companies, no shared keys by design, confirmed by Voja).

Cross-referenced against the manual SWC_Structured_BOM.xlsx (sibling SWC cabin, hand-built by Voja): only 50% of that file's 'Part Number' values are a clean Document Number match; the remainder are manufacturer/catalog part numbers or ad-hoc annotated strings, evidencing that a single 'part number' field gets overloaded with whichever identifier was available at extraction time -- see OQ-68.
